content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 17 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Hebrew Base:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), LC PDF pages 214–215 | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pa_0154
**Verse count:** 20

---

**1** You shall not sacrifice to the LORD your God an ox or a sheep in which there is a blemish, any defect whatever, for that is an abomination to the LORD your God.

**2** If there is found among you, within any of your gates which the LORD your God is giving you, a man or a woman who does what is evil in the eyes of the LORD your God, by transgressing his covenant—

**3** and has gone and served other gods and bowed down to them, or to the sun or to the moon or to any of the host of heaven, which I have not commanded—

**4** and it is told to you and you hear it, and you inquire diligently; and behold, if the matter is true and certain—this abomination was done in Israel—

**5** then you shall bring out that man or that woman who has done this evil thing to your gates, and you shall stone that man or that woman to death with stones.

**6** On the evidence of two witnesses or three witnesses the one who is to die shall be put to death; he shall not be put to death on the evidence of one witness.

**7** The hand of the witnesses shall be upon him first to put him to death, and afterward the hand of all the people; and you shall burn out the evil from your midst.

**8** If any case is too difficult for you in judgment—between one kind of bloodshed and another, between one kind of legal claim and another, between one kind of assault and another, matters of dispute in your gates—then you shall arise and go up to the place which the LORD your God will choose.

**9** And you shall come to the Levitical priests and to the judge who will be in office in those days, and you shall inquire, and they shall declare to you the decision of the case.

**10** Then you shall do according to the word which they declare to you from that place which the LORD will choose, and you shall be careful to do according to all that they instruct you.

**11** According to the instruction [torah] which they give you, and according to the decision which they declare to you, you shall do; you shall not turn aside from the word which they declare to you, to the right or to the left.

**12** And the man who acts presumptuously, so as not to listen to the priest who stands to minister there before the LORD your God, or to the judge—that man shall die; and you shall burn out the evil from Israel.

**13** And all the people shall hear and fear and shall not act presumptuously again.

**14** When you come to the land which the LORD your God is giving you and you possess it and dwell in it, and you say, "I will set a king over me, like all the nations that are around me"—

**15** you shall indeed set over yourself a king whom the LORD your God will choose; from among your brothers you shall set a king over you; you may not put a foreigner [ish nokri] over you, who is not your brother.

**16** Only he must not multiply horses for himself, or cause the people to return to Egypt in order to multiply horses, since the LORD has said to you, "You shall never return that way again."

**17** And he must not multiply wives for himself, lest his heart turn away; nor shall he acquire excessive silver and gold for himself.

**18** And when he sits on the throne of his kingdom, he shall write for himself a copy of this instruction [mishneh ha-torah ha-zot] in a scroll, from before the Levitical priests.

**19** And it shall be with him, and he shall read in it all the days of his life, so that he may learn to fear the LORD his God by keeping all the words of this instruction and these statutes, to do them—

**20** so that his heart may not be lifted up above his brothers, and so that he does not turn aside from the commandment to the right or to the left, so that he may prolong his days in his kingdom, he and his children, in the midst of Israel.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**v.1 — "any defect whatever":** LOGOS's expanded rendering "any defect whatever" for *kol davar ra'* ("any evil thing") is clearest; EMET's "any evil thing" is literal but could mislead. SOPHIA adopts "any defect whatever" (LOGOS).

**v.3 — "host of heaven":** All three agents agree on this rendering of *tseva ha-shamayim*. SOPHIA retains "host of heaven" as the established biblical idiom.

**v.8 — triadic distinctions:** EMET renders literally: "between blood and blood, between plea and plea, between stroke and stroke." LOGOS expands to "bloodshed," "legal right," "assault." SOPHIA adopts a middle path: "one kind of bloodshed and another, one kind of legal claim and another, one kind of assault and another" — preserving the triadic structure with natural English.

**v.11 — torah:** SOPHIA retains the bracketed gloss [instruction] on *torah* per project convention whenever the word appears in its primary sense.

**v.15 — ish nokri:** SOPHIA retains the Hebrew term in brackets. The distinction *nokri* (foreign/alien) vs. *ach* (brother/Israelite) is legally and covenantally significant in the royal law.

**v.18 — mishneh ha-torah ha-zot:** SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew phrase in brackets as [copy of this instruction] and notes in the Council that this is the phrase from which the LXX coined *Deuteronomion* ("second law"), giving the book its Greek/English name. The king writes his personal Torah scroll — the political theology of the passage is significant.

**v.20 — "prolong his days":** EMET reads "prolong [days] in his kingdom"; LOGOS "continue long in his kingdom"; PNEUMA "reign for a long time." SOPHIA adopts "prolong his days in his kingdom" as the most literal rendering of *ya'arich yamim*.

*— SOPHIA*
